citations: Chetty (2012) on taxable income elasticities and small intensive-margin labor supply responses

runs.reasoningSummary: My belief is that prime-age primary earners in the middle of the earnings distribution have relatively low intensive-margin substitution elasticities in U.S. tax-benefit models, typically around 0.1–0.2, with decile 5 not especially atypical.
